name: Chicken and Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 2 broiler-fryer chickens (2 1/2-3 lbs each), cut up
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour in a bowl or bag; add the chicken pieces and dredge or shake to coat.
2. In a large skillet, brown the chicken in oil; drain.
3. Transfer the chicken to an 8-quart Dutch oven.
4. Add celery, carrots, parsley, and seasonings. Add enough water to cover the chicken; bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat; cover and simmer until the chicken is almost tender, about 45-50 minutes.
6. Remove 1 cup of broth to use for dumplings; cool, then add flour, baking powder, and eggs. Mix well to form a stiff batter.
7. Drop by tablespoonfuls into the simmering broth. Cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
8. Remove chicken and dumplings to a serving dish and keep warm.
9. For gravy, remove 4 cups of broth and vegetables to a large saucepan; bring to a boil.
10. Combine flour and water; mix well. Stir into the vegetable mixture. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly.
11. Pour the gravy over chicken and dumplings. Serve immediately.
